mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the predictive IC drops below zero during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDP growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 10-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with accelerating inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0.1% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during identified stagflationary periods.
